quality.rationale: 多步张量缩并与曲率推导，结合高斯法坐标与外曲率性质，导向ADM初值方程

origin.question: Problem 9.33. Using the results of Problem 9.32, derive expressions for ${}^{(4)}\mathbf{G}_{\beta}^{\alpha}$ , the components of the Einstein tensor, in Gaussian normal coordinates.
origin.solution: Solution 9.33. The Riemann components were found in Problem 9.32. Contraction gives$$\begin{array}{l} {}^{( 4 )} \mathrm {R}_{j}^{i} = g^{i k} ( {}^{( 4 )} \mathrm {R}_{k n j}^{n} + {}^{( 4 )} \mathrm {R}_{k m j}^{m} ) \\=^{(3)} \mathrm {R}_{\mathrm{j}}^{\mathrm{i}} + \varepsilon \left(\mathrm {g}^{\mathrm{i k}} \mathrm {K}_{\mathrm{k j}, \mathrm{n}} + 2 \mathrm {K}_{\mathrm{m}}^{\mathrm{i}} \mathrm {K}_{\mathrm{j}}^{\mathrm{m}} - \mathrm {K}_{\mathrm{j}}^{\mathrm{i}} \mathrm {K}\right) \tag {1} \\\end{array}$$where $\mathbf{K} \equiv \mathbf {K}_{\mathbf {i}}^{\mathbf {i}}$ . Now $\mathbf {g}_{\mathbf {\mu},\mathbf {n}}^{\mathbf {ik}} = -\mathbf {g}_{\mathbf {\mu},\mathbf {n}}^{\mathbf {im}}\mathbf {g}_{\mathbf {\mu},\mathbf {s}}^{\mathbf {ks}}\mathbf {g}_{\mathbf {\mu},\mathbf {n}}$ (see e.g. Problem 21.1) and $-\mathbf {g}_{\mathbf {ms},\mathbf {n}} = 2\mathbf {K}_{\mathbf {ms}}$ in Gaussian normal coordinates, so$$\mathrm {g}_{, \mathrm {n}}^{\mathrm {i k}} = 2 \mathrm {g}^{\mathrm {i m}} \mathrm {g}^{\mathrm {k s}} \mathrm {K}_{\mathrm {m s}} = 2 \mathrm {K}^{\mathrm {i k}}.$$Equation (1) can now be rewritten:$$\begin{array}{l} \left(^{(4)} \mathrm {R}_{\mathrm {j}}^{\mathrm {i}} =\right.^{(3)} \mathrm {R}_{\mathrm {j}}^{\mathrm {i}} + \varepsilon \left(\mathrm {g}^{\mathrm {i k}} \mathrm {K}_{\mathrm {k j}, \mathrm {n}} + \mathrm {g}^{\mathrm {i k}}_{\mathrm {, n}} \mathrm {K}_{\mathrm {k j}} - \mathrm {K}_{\mathrm {j}}^{\mathrm {i}} \mathrm {K}\right) \\=^{(3)} \mathrm {R}_{\mathrm {j}}^{\mathrm {i}} + \varepsilon \left(\mathrm {K}_{\mathrm {j}, \mathrm {n}}^{\mathrm {i}} - \mathrm {K}_{\mathrm {j}}^{\mathrm {i}} \mathrm {K}\right). \tag {2}. \\\end{array}$$For the other components of the Ricci tensor we have:$$\left(^{(4)} \mathrm {R}_{\mathrm {j}}^{\mathrm {n}} = \varepsilon^{(4)} \mathrm {R}_{\mathrm {n a j}}^{\alpha}\right) = -^{(4)} \mathrm {R}_{\mathrm {i j}}^{\mathrm {n i}} = \varepsilon \left(\mathrm {K}_{\left| \mathrm {j}\right.} - \mathrm {K}_{\left. \mathrm {j}\right| \mathrm {i}}^{\mathrm {i}}\right) \tag {3}$$$$\left(^{(4)} \mathrm {R}_{\mathrm {n}}^{\mathrm {n}} = \varepsilon^{(4)} \mathrm {R}_{\mathrm {n i n}}^{\mathrm {i}} = \varepsilon \mathrm {g}^{\mathrm {i j}} \left(\mathrm {K}_{\mathrm {i j}, \mathrm {n}} + \mathrm {K}_{\mathrm {i m}} \mathrm {K}_{\mathrm {j}}^{\mathrm {m}}\right) = \varepsilon \left(\mathrm {K}_{\mathrm {, n}} - \mathrm {K}_{\mathrm {i m}} \mathrm {K}^{\mathrm {m i}}\right).\right. \tag {4}$$The Ricci scalar is then$$\left(^{(4)} \mathbf {R} =^{(4)} \mathbf {R}_{\mathrm {n}}^{\mathrm {n}} +^{(4)} \mathbf {R}_{\mathrm {i}}^{\mathrm {i}} =^{(3)} \mathbf {R} + \varepsilon \left(2 \mathrm {K}_{\mathrm {, n}} - \mathrm {K}_{\mathrm {i m}} \mathrm {K}^{\mathrm {i m}} - \mathrm {K}^{2}\right)\right. \tag {5}$$and the components of the Einstein tensor are:$$G_{n}^{n} =^{(4)} R_{n}^{n} - \frac {1}{2}^{(4)} R = - \frac {1}{2}^{(3)} R + \frac {1}{2} \varepsilon \left(K^{2} - K_{\mathrm {i m}} K^{\mathrm {i m}}\right) \tag {6}$$$$\mathrm {G}_{\mathrm {j}}^{\mathrm {n}} = \mathrm {(4)} \mathrm {R}_{\mathrm {j}}^{\mathrm {n}} = \varepsilon \left(\mathrm {K}_{\left| \mathrm {j} - \mathrm {K}_{\mathrm {j} \mid \mathrm {i}}^{\mathrm {i}}\right)}\right) \tag {7}$$$$\begin{array}{l} \mathbf {G}_{j}^{i} = \mathbf {\delta}^{(4)} \mathbf {R}_{j}^{i} - \frac {1}{2} \delta_{j}^{i} \mathbf {\delta}^{(4)} \mathbf {R} \\=^{(3)} \mathrm {G}_{j}^{i} + \varepsilon \left[ \mathrm {K}_{j, n}^{i} - \mathrm {K}_{j}^{i} K - \frac {1}{2} \delta_{j}^{i} \left(2 \mathrm {K}_{, n} - \mathrm {K}_{\mathrm {i m}} \mathrm {K}^{\mathrm {i m}} - \mathrm {K}^{2}\right)\right]. \tag {8}. \\\end{array}$$Equating (6) and (7) to the corresponding components of the stress-energy tensor gives \"initial value equations\" for the gravitational field.
